# Implementation Readiness Checklist: Live Integration Tests

**Purpose**: Requirements-quality gate for weaker LLM implementers — completeness, clarity, consistency, measurability, and scenario coverage of feature 003 artifacts (not implementation behavior).
**Created**: 2026-07-18
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md) · [plan.md](../plan.md) · [tasks.md](../tasks.md) · [contracts/live-test-interface.md](../contracts/live-test-interface.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements. Every item tests whether the English/spec artifacts are unambiguous enough to implement without discretionary forks.

**Audience**: Author + PR reviewer  
**Depth**: Standard-to-rigorous release gate for requirements quality  
**Focus**: Unambiguous decisions; presence/contracts/env/CI/cleanup/markers; edge/exception/recovery coverage in requirements text

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are exact public SDK exception type names required for auth, access, not-found, validation, transport, and process failures stated (not only “categories”)? [Completeness, Gap, Spec §US-004, Spec §FR-014–FR-015, Plan §7.5]
- [ ] CHK002 Are exact project fields (names + types) for omitted / explicit-null / empty-string / empty-collection partial-update cases enumerated with expected backend outcomes? [Completeness, Gap, Spec §US-003, Spec §FR-012–FR-013, Plan §7.3, Tasks T042–T044]
- [ ] CHK003 Is the smoke “simple CRUD resource” named as a hard requirement (e.g. labels) in the spec, not only in the plan/tasks? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009, Plan §7.2, Ambiguity]
- [ ] CHK004 Are exact env-var names, required/optional matrix, defaults, and fail-closed setup-error (not skip) outcomes complete for every launch mode (compose, resolver, existing URL)? [Completeness, Contract §1, Plan §5.1, Quickstart §4]
- [ ] CHK005 Are numeric bounds documented for readiness timeout, poll interval, artifact retention, and diagnostic log size (not only “bounded” / “short”)? [Completeness, Gap, Contract §1/§6/§8, Spec §NFR-003, Plan §5.3]
- [ ] CHK006 Is the exact `/readyz` success body (or “any 2xx with empty/ignored body”) specified so readiness cannot be implemented two ways? [Completeness, Gap, Contract §6, Plan §5.3, Spec §FR-005]
- [ ] CHK007 Are marker semantics complete: does `live_smoke`/`live_extended` imply `live`, and is default `pytest` exclusion exactly `not live` vs directory-only? [Completeness, Plan §6, Research §R-009, Tasks T002]
- [ ] CHK008 Are recovery/cleanup postconditions after partial workflow failure, cleanup failure after pass, and cleanup failure after fail fully specified as observable session outcomes? [Completeness, Spec §FR-019–FR-020, Contract §4, Data-model §2]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK009 Is FR-010’s condition “если эти возможности доступны в целевой версии” resolved to skip / fail-closed / pin-required, with no implementer choice? [Clarity, Ambiguity, Spec §FR-010, Spec §US-001]
- [ ] CHK010 Is FR-008’s “текущего или доступного workspace” reduced to one exact public API call and assertion target? [Clarity, Ambiguity, Spec §FR-008, Plan §7.1, Tasks T029]
- [ ] CHK011 Is FR-020’s hedge “насколько это технически возможно” replaced by an explicit ordered cleanup contract and non-negotiable postconditions? [Clarity, Ambiguity, Spec §FR-020, Plan §5.7, Spec §SC-005]
- [ ] CHK012 Is US-003 / FR-012 “согласно фактическому контракту Multica” bound to pinned expected outcomes for this feature’s target (not deferred discovery at implement time)? [Clarity, Ambiguity, Spec §US-003, Spec §FR-012, Spec §SC-008]
- [ ] CHK013 Is NFR-006 “достаточно компактным” reconciled to the measurable SC-003 five-minute gate (or an explicit dual soft/hard criterion)? [Clarity, Spec §NFR-006, Spec §SC-003, Plan §9]
- [ ] CHK014 Does Contract §1 choose exactly one CI policy for `MULTICA_LIVE_KEEP_ENV` (“игнорируется” XOR “запрещается”), not both? [Clarity, Ambiguity, Conflict, Contract §1]
- [ ] CHK015 Is “resolver enabled” defined with exact env/flag/command that makes `MULTICA_LIVE_CLI` optional? [Clarity, Gap, Contract §1, Tasks T007–T008]
- [ ] CHK016 Are placeholder TOML values (`vX.Y.Z`, `sha256:...`) labeled as documentation examples only, with a rule that the real manifest must contain concrete non-placeholder values before merge? [Clarity, Contract §2, Tasks T003, Plan §10]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK017 Do Spec FR-019’s “resource delete OR environment destroy” and Plan layered cleanup (registry → compose down -v → audit) state one mandatory cleanup hierarchy without an exclusive-or fork? [Consistency, Conflict, Spec §FR-019, Plan §5.7/§15]
- [ ] CHK018 Is MVP scope consistent: Spec §13 includes FR-028 / US-007 in minimal release while Tasks MVP lists only T068–T070 and defers US6/T071 — is FR-028 fully in or partially deferred? [Consistency, Conflict, Spec §13, Tasks §MVP, Spec §FR-028]
- [ ] CHK019 Does Plan §12’s claim that `memory/constitution.md` is absent match the repository (constitution exists at `.specify/memory/constitution.md`), and is the Constitution Check result updated accordingly? [Consistency, Conflict, Plan §12, Constitution]
- [ ] CHK020 Are artifact-upload rules consistent between “only on failure” and “always with short retention” across Plan §9 and Contract §8? [Consistency, Ambiguity, Plan §9, Contract §8]
- [ ] CHK021 Do default pytest exclusion (`not live`), smoke selector (`live_smoke`), and extended selector (`live_smoke or live_extended`) form one coherent marker matrix without contradictory “MODE=extended” semantics? [Consistency, Plan §6, Contract §8, Quickstart §5]
- [ ] CHK022 Is “простой ресурс” / labels CRUD aligned across Spec FR-009, Plan §7.2, and Tasks Phase 4 without alternate resource substitution left open? [Consistency, Spec §FR-009, Plan §7.2, Tasks T035]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK023 Can SC-004 (“десять последовательных запусков… без нестабильных падений”) be objectively scored (exact command, flaky definition, allowed retries = 0)? [Measurability, Spec §SC-004, Tasks T075, Plan §14]
- [ ] CHK024 Can SC-005 cleanup success be verified without secrets using named postconditions (containers, volumes, HOME, profile path)? [Measurability, Spec §SC-005, Contract §5, Plan §5.3, Tasks T026/T058]
- [ ] CHK025 Can SC-008 / SC-009 be verified from written expected values (byte-identical text, field-level omit/null/empty matrix) rather than “кроме явно документированного поведения Multica” alone? [Measurability, Ambiguity, Spec §SC-008–SC-009, Spec §US-002]
- [ ] CHK026 Are US-001 acceptance criteria free of unmeasured phrases (“диагностируемую ошибку подготовки”) by naming the setup-failure class/message contract? [Measurability, Spec §US-001, Contract §4]
- [ ] CHK027 Is intentional contract-mutation gate (SC-002 / Tasks T071) specified enough to know which mutations are mandatory for MVP vs follow-up? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002, Tasks §MVP, Tasks T071]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK028 Are primary happy-path requirements complete for bootstrap → workspace read → CRUD → issue workflow → isolation as a single blocking smoke story? [Coverage, Spec §US-001–US-002/US-005, Spec §SC-001]
- [ ] CHK029 Are exception-path requirements complete for invalid PAT, inaccessible workspace, missing resource, validation error, closed port, and mid-operation backend death? [Coverage, Spec §US-004, Spec §FR-014–FR-015, Plan §7.5]
- [ ] CHK030 Are recovery requirements defined for: cleanup after assertion failure; cleanup failure after pass; KEEP_ENV local debug vs CI forbid; re-run after forced failure? [Coverage, Gap, Spec §FR-020, Contract §4, Plan §14, Tasks T058]
- [ ] CHK031 Are alternate-path requirements defined for `MULTICA_LIVE_EXISTING_URL` (local reuse) versus full Compose lifecycle, including which cleanup layers still apply? [Coverage, Gap, Contract §1, Plan §5.7, Spec §FR-019]
- [ ] CHK032 Are extended-suite requirements explicit about pinned-target regression vs upstream-main non-blocking signal (pass/fail interpretation)? [Coverage, Spec §US-006, Spec §12, Plan §9, Tasks T067]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK033 Do requirements state expected harness behavior for each Spec §9 edge (not-ready backend, CLI stdout+JSON mix, nonzero exit without JSON, empty page / repeated cursor, zero-byte attachment, Unicode filenames)? [Coverage, Spec §9, Spec §FR-015/FR-025/FR-027]
- [ ] CHK034 Is presence-semantics matrix complete for response JSON: absent key vs `null` vs `""` vs `[]` vs `{}` vs unknown extra fields, including whether SDK decode must accept or reject each? [Coverage, Spec §9, Spec §FR-026, Data-model §9, Tasks T061]
- [ ] CHK035 Are parallel-run and serial/destructive marker requirements sufficient to forbid incompatible interpretations of Spec §9 “два тестовых запуска параллельно” under Plan’s no-xdist rule? [Coverage, Spec §9, Plan §6, Research §R-010, Tasks T050/T055]
- [ ] CHK036 When resource cleanup is impossible mid-workflow, is the required fallback (environment destroy + diagnostic note) mandatory and measurable? [Edge Case, Spec §9, Spec §FR-019–FR-020, Plan §15]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK037 Are Assumptions §11 items that gate implementability (noninteractive dev-auth, compose file name, `/readyz` availability) marked as hard prerequisites with fail-closed setup errors if unmet? [Assumption, Spec §11, Contract §4, Research §R-003–R-004]
- [ ] CHK038 Is dependency on official Compose service names (`postgres`, `backend`) and compose filename pinned so upstream rename is a target-update event, not silent implementer adaptation? [Dependency, Plan §5.3, Contract §2, Plan §15]
- [ ] CHK039 Is the test-only `httpx` dependency and “must not become runtime dependency” constraint stated as a non-negotiable packaging requirement aligned with the constitution? [Dependency, Plan §2/§5.4, Research §R-006, Constitution §SDK Constraints]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts / Unselected Alternatives

- [ ] CHK040 Does any remaining “should/may/or/appropriate/as needed/compatible/etc./TBD/later/возможно пересмотреть” in Spec/Plan/Research/Contract leave an implementer-discretion fork for MVP scope? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-010/FR-019/FR-020/FR-027, Contract §1, Research §R-001, Plan §9]
- [ ] CHK041 For FR-027 attachments (“при наличии поддерживаемых”), is the MVP/extended decision skip-with-report vs fail vs pin-required chosen explicitly? [Unselected Alternative, Spec §FR-027, Plan §8, Tasks T063]
- [ ] CHK042 Are Research alternatives (Testcontainers, pytest-docker, urllib, xdist, rename `tests/integration`) explicitly closed for this feature’s MVP so implementers cannot re-open them? [Unselected Alternative, Research §R-001/R-006/R-009/R-010, Plan §4]
- [ ] CHK043 Is the Python CI matrix decision final (blocking live = 3.12 only) while local support remains 3.12+3.13, without implying implementers must matrix live jobs? [Ambiguity, Plan §2/§9, Constitution §SDK Constraints]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline when an item fails; prefer fixing Spec/Plan/Contract/Tasks over coding around ambiguity
- This checklist is a requirements-quality gate; do not use it as an implementation test plan
- Soft-cap note: items prioritize weaker-implementer risk (presence semantics, env/CI, cleanup, markers, exception names, unselected ors)

---

## Iteration 2 — Residual Risks (2026-07-18)

**Purpose**: Post-remediation requirements-quality gate. Prior CHK001–CHK043 themes were largely addressed in Spec/Plan/Tasks/Contracts (exception names file, Unset matrix, markers/addopts, KEEP_ENV forbid, artifact `if: failure()` + 7d, layered cleanup, labels CRUD, FR hedges, MVP T071/T075, constitution PASS, v0.3.35 pin). Items below cover **remaining** implementer-discretion forks only.

**Audience**: Author + PR reviewer  
**Depth**: Rigorous residual gate for weaker-model `/speckit-implement`

### Residual Completeness

- [ ] CHK044 Is the exact HTTP workspace scoping mechanism for oracle/bootstrap calls named (header name + value source), not “workspace header/context required by upstream”? [Completeness, Gap, Ambiguity, Contract bootstrap-http §4]
- [ ] CHK045 Is the exact CLI profile `config.json` schema for pinned `v0.3.35` documented (required keys, types, nesting), or is “format expected by current CLI” still implementer discovery? [Completeness, Gap, Plan §5.5, Data-model §6, Tasks T016]
- [ ] CHK046 Does bootstrap contract state which response fields yield `TestIdentity.user_id` / workspace `id` (JSON paths), not only that `token`/`id` exist? [Completeness, Gap, Contract bootstrap-http §2, Data-model §4–§5]
- [ ] CHK047 Are attachment HTTP routes (upload/metadata/download/delete) pinned in `bootstrap-http.md` before US6 implementation, matching Contract §11 payloads? [Completeness, Gap, Spec §FR-027, Contract live-test-interface §11, Tasks T063]
- [ ] CHK048 Are pagination/filter query parameter and cursor field names for issues pinned (not only `page_size=10` and “status + label id”)? [Completeness, Gap, Contract live-test-interface §11, Tasks T059–T060]

### Residual Clarity / Unselected Alternatives

- [ ] CHK049 Is access-collapse mapping a single expected public exception for the pinned target, rather than “`AuthorizationError` or `NotFoundError` … pin once observed”? [Clarity, Unselected Alternative, Spec §US-004, Contract presence-and-exceptions §3]
- [ ] CHK050 Is mid-operation backend-death mapping a single expected public exception (`NetworkError` XOR `CommandExecutionError`), not an open OR? [Clarity, Unselected Alternative, Contract presence-and-exceptions §3, Tasks T050]
- [ ] CHK051 Does bootstrap step 4 choose exactly one auth credential (JWT XOR PAT) for `POST /api/workspaces`, not “Bearer JWT or PAT”? [Clarity, Unselected Alternative, Contract bootstrap-http §2]
- [ ] CHK052 Is `MULTICA_LIVE_KEEP_ENV` truthiness fully defined (`1` only vs any non-empty/`true`/`yes`) and is CI detection exact (`CI=true` / GitHub Actions)? [Clarity, Ambiguity, Contract live-test-interface §1, Tasks T007]
- [ ] CHK053 Does T071 name the concrete mutation targets (exact CLI flag string, exact response field, exact not-found mapping locus) rather than categories alone? [Clarity, Gap, Spec §SC-002, Tasks T071]
- [ ] CHK054 Is Spec §9 “CLI writes diagnostics to stdout with JSON” given a required harness/SDK outcome (decode success / fail class), or intentionally out of MVP? [Clarity, Coverage, Spec §9, Spec §FR-015]

### Residual Consistency

- [ ] CHK055 Is SC-003 a hard fail gate or a soft budget? Spec/Plan say must/enforced while Contract §10 labels it “soft runtime budget” under a 10-minute job timeout. [Consistency, Conflict, Spec §NFR-006/§SC-003, Plan §9, Contract live-test-interface §10]
- [ ] CHK056 Is `backend_digest` required non-null for blocking/CI targets consistently across Contract §2 (required, no placeholders) and Data-model §1 (optional/null)? [Consistency, Conflict, Contract live-test-interface §2, Data-model §1, Tasks T003]
- [ ] CHK057 Does the Contract §2 example still containing `sha256:<resolved-before-merge>` conflict with the “placeholders forbidden in committed file” rule in a way that invites committing the example literally? [Consistency, Ambiguity, Contract live-test-interface §2, Tasks T003, Checklist requirements.md]
- [ ] CHK058 Is feature readiness status consistent (Spec §status Draft vs Plan/Tasks “Ready for implementation”) so implementers know which artifacts are authoritative? [Consistency, Spec header, Plan header, Tasks header]

### Residual Scenario / Edge Coverage

- [ ] CHK059 For `MULTICA_LIVE_EXISTING_URL`, are bootstrap/profile/oracle/readiness requirements complete (which steps still run, which Compose/env allowlist steps are skipped)? [Coverage, Gap, Contract live-test-interface §1/§9, Plan §5.1/§5.3]
- [ ] CHK060 Are expected outcomes for Spec §9 pagination edges (empty page, repeated cursor, changing set) specified as assert/fail/skip, or explicitly deferred with US6? [Coverage, Ambiguity, Spec §9, Contract live-test-interface §11, Tasks T059]
- [ ] CHK061 Is T044’s file-location OR (`test_projects.py` or `test_issue_workflow.py`) closed to one mandatory path for case `C-EMPTY`? [Ambiguity, Tasks T044, Contract presence-and-exceptions §2]

### Residual Dependencies & Closed Alternatives

- [ ] CHK062 Is Research R-006’s “urllib допустим для bootstrap-only MVP” explicitly superseded/rejected so implementers cannot choose urllib over the plan’s `httpx` test-only decision? [Unselected Alternative, Research §R-006, Plan §2/§5.4]
- [ ] CHK063 Are numeric `httpx` timeouts for bootstrap/oracle/readiness HTTP calls specified (connect/read/total), not only “explicit timeouts”? [Completeness, Gap, Tasks T018, Contract bootstrap-http §2–§4]

### Iteration 2 Notes

- Prefer fixing residual contract pins (workspace header, profile schema, exception XOR, SC-003 hard/soft, digest example) before `/speckit-implement` by a weaker model
- US6 gaps (CHK047/CHK048/CHK060) may remain open if extended suite stays post-MVP, but must be labeled as deferred rather than left discoverable
- Do not treat CHK001–CHK043 as still-open remediation themes unless re-checked failed after this iteration
